Black Swan (2010)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $107,770,258Details
International Box Office $224,312,032
Worldwide Box Office $332,082,290
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $20,374,382
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $9,820,385
Total $30,194,767
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Nina, a featured dancer at the New York Ballet, finds herself caught in a web of competitive intrigue with Lily, a new dancer at the company. As the two dancers expand their rivalry into a twisted friendship, Nina begins to get more in touch with her dark side with a recklessness that threatens to destroy her.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$1,443,809 (1.3% of total gross)
Legs:12.13 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$13,000,000 (worldwide box office is 25.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:18 opening theaters/2,407 max. theaters, 7.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $162,489,819

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 3rd, 2010 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
December 17th, 2010 (Expands Wide) by Fox Searchlight
August 21st, 2025 (IMAX) by IMAX Films
International Releases: January 20th, 2011 (Wide) (Australia)
February 24th, 2011 (Wide) (South Korea)
November 7th, 2024 (Limited) (Australia)
Video Release: March 29th, 2011 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong sexual content, disturbing violent images, language and some drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2133, 8/11/2010)
Running Time: 108 minutes
Award nomination: 2011 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Plot point: Dancing, Dysfunctional Family, Hallucinations, LGBTQ+, Mental Illness
Subgenre: Psychological Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Dune Entertainment, Cross Creek Pictures, Phoenix Pictures, Protozoa Pictures, Mad Chance
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Nina, a featured dancer at the New York Ballet, finds herself caught in a web of competitive intrigue with Lily, a new dancer at the company. As the two dancers expand their rivalry into a twisted friendship, Nina begins to get more in touch with her dark side with a recklessness that threatens to destroy her.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$1,443,809 (1.3% of total gross)
Legs:12.13 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$13,000,000 (worldwide box office is 25.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:18 opening theaters/2,407 max. theaters, 7.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $162,489,819

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 3rd, 2010 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
December 17th, 2010 (Expands Wide) by Fox Searchlight
August 21st, 2025 (IMAX) by IMAX Films

February 24th, 2011 (Wide) (South Korea)
November 7th, 2024 (Limited) (Australia)
Video Release: March 29th, 2011 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong sexual content, disturbing violent images, language and some drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2133, 8/11/2010)
